page: Amazon Web Services c8i.metal-96xl is a bare-metal, compute-optimized server powered by Intel Xeon 6975P-C processors. Utilizing the x86_64 architecture, this instance features 192 physical cores and 384 vCPUs with hyper-threading enabled, running at a clock speed of 3.9 GHz. It is equipped with 768.0 GB of high-speed DDR5 memory operating at 7 GHz, providing 2.0 GB of RAM per core. The processor includes a massive 480 MB L3 cache and supports advanced instruction sets such as Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX) and Advanced Vector Extensions (AVX-512) for accelerated matrix and vector operations.

In benchmark evaluations, the c8i.metal-96xl demonstrates top-tier multi-core performance. It achieves exceptional results in multi-threaded tasks, including a top-10% rating in stress-ng multi-core CPU testing and passmark CPU scores. Memory bandwidth is highly efficient, reaching 1170.89 GB/sec for large-block read operations. The server also excels in database operations, registering 510.74 million operations per second in Redis SET benchmarks, and delivers outstanding static web serving throughput at 178.42 GB/sec. However, single-core performance and text generation speeds for small language models fall into average or lower tiers, highlighting a clear optimization for highly parallelized workloads over single-threaded tasks.

This bare-metal instance does not include local NVMe storage, requiring network-attached storage solutions, but features a robust 100 Gbps baseline network bandwidth. From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the c8i.metal-96xl offers high resource density and exceptional compute-per-resource value for enterprise-scale deployments. It is highly suited for compute-intensive workloads such as large-scale software compilation, high-performance computing (HPC), cryptography, database operations, and multi-threaded data processing.
description: Amazon Web Services c8i.metal-96xl is a bare-metal, compute-optimized server featuring 192 physical cores and 384 vCPUs powered by Intel Xeon 6975P-C processors running at 3.9 GHz. It is equipped with 768.0 GB of DDR5 memory at 7 GHz and a 100 Gbps baseline network interface, though it lacks local storage. The platform excels in highly parallelized workloads, delivering top-tier multi-core CPU performance, exceptional memory bandwidth, and outstanding database operations. It supports advanced instruction sets like Intel AMX and AVX-512, making it highly efficient for vector and matrix calculations. This instance is optimized for high-performance computing, large-scale software compilation, cryptography, and enterprise database workloads.
og_description: Amazon Web Services c8i.metal-96xl is a bare-metal, compute-optimized server featuring 384 vCPUs, 768 GB DDR5 RAM, and 100 Gbps network bandwidth, optimized for highly parallel enterprise workloads.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services c8i.metal-96xl is a bare-metal server with 384 vCPUs, 768 GB DDR5 RAM, and 100 Gbps network bandwidth for compute-heavy workloads.
tagline: A high-density bare-metal compute platform featuring 384 vCPUs and DDR5 memory for demanding parallel processing and enterprise workloads.

Equipped with 768.0 GB of high-speed DDR5 memory and 100 Gbps baseline network bandwidth
